project.name: gddp-config
project.description: Source of truth for the Graph-Driven Agentic Development (GDDP) system, defining the schemas, graphs, templates, and human-owned project truth consumed by the runtime and executors. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: acceptance_items.py
nodes.summary: Normalizes human-authored acceptance criteria into stable keyed objects, preserving readable criterion text while enforcing unique kebab-case identifiers.

nodes.name: slugify_acceptance_id
nodes.summary: Converts criterion text into a bounded kebab-case identifier with a safe alphabetic fallback.

nodes.name: make_acceptance_item
nodes.summary: Builds a keyed acceptance item and adds numeric suffixes until its identifier is unique among existing criteria.

nodes.name: normalize_acceptance_items
nodes.summary: Converts mixed legacy strings and keyed objects into a normalized list with valid, unique criterion identifiers.

nodes.name: acceptance_text
nodes.summary: Extracts non-empty criterion text from either a keyed acceptance object or a legacy string.

nodes.name: acceptance_has_placeholder
nodes.summary: Detects unresolved REPLACE_ME markers across acceptance-criteria collections.

nodes.name: batch_fill.py
nodes.summary: Provides the interactive batch editor that finds placeholder node fields, guides human input, validates completed graph nodes, and writes approved YAML updates.

nodes.name: list_node_ids
nodes.summary: Lists graph node identifiers from a project's YAML node directory in deterministic order.

nodes.name: gather_inspiration_bullets
nodes.summary: Collects unique, non-placeholder acceptance and constraint text from existing project nodes for reuse as authoring suggestions.

nodes.name: needs_batch
nodes.summary: Determines whether a node still has placeholder or empty human-authored fields requiring batch completion.

nodes.name: show_node_card
nodes.summary: Renders the current node's identity, incomplete fields, dependencies, and unlocks as an interactive Rich panel.

nodes.name: manual_text
nodes.summary: Captures single-line or multi-line human text for an unresolved node field.

nodes.name: edit_list_items
nodes.summary: Runs the interactive add, delete, suggest, and finish loop for acceptance criteria or constraints.

nodes.name: fill_node_fields
nodes.summary: Fills placeholder why, acceptance, and constraint fields using manual input and reusable project suggestions.

nodes.name: review_and_write
nodes.summary: Presents a completed node for human review, writes accepted YAML, runs validation, and supports bounded field edits or retries.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Drives the project-wide batch-fill session across incomplete nodes and reports written, skipped, and total counts.

nodes.name: rapid_add.py
nodes.summary: Implements the minimal-keystroke node creation flow, including dependency selection, optional LLM drafting, node YAML creation, and project graph patching.

nodes.name: slugify
nodes.summary: Normalizes free-form node names to valid kebab-case identifiers and rejects invalid results.

nodes.name: kebab_to_title
nodes.summary: Converts a kebab-case node identifier into a display title.

nodes.name: list_existing_nodes
nodes.summary: Lists existing node identifiers for dependency selection and duplicate prevention.

nodes.name: pick_deps
nodes.summary: Provides a paginated single-key dependency picker with selection toggles and in-place redraws.

nodes.name: make_node_dict
nodes.summary: Creates a schema-shaped pending capability node with selected dependencies and placeholder human-authored fields.

nodes.name: render_node_yaml
nodes.summary: Serializes a node in canonical field order as readable YAML.

nodes.name: write_node
nodes.summary: Creates the project node directory and writes a rendered node YAML file.

nodes.name: patch_project_yaml
nodes.summary: Backs up and atomically attempts to append a new node summary to project.yaml, restoring the original on failure.

nodes.name: ensure_project_shell
nodes.summary: Creates a minimal project graph shell with blueprint placeholders and conservative execution policy when none exists.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates rapid node naming, dependency selection, optional LLM field drafting, graph writes, and next-step guidance.

nodes.name: terminal.py
nodes.summary: Supplies shared terminal primitives for single-key input, arrow-key decoding, line input, and in-place menu redraws across the node-authoring tools.

nodes.name: clear_lines
nodes.summary: Moves the cursor upward and clears the remaining screen region so interactive menus can redraw in place.

nodes.name: getch
nodes.summary: Reads one terminal keypress, decodes arrow sequences, preserves Ctrl-C, and falls back to /dev/tty for piped stdin.

nodes.name: getline
nodes.summary: Reads a prompted text line from stdin or /dev/tty while safely handling EOF and terminal cleanup.

nodes.name: import_node.py
nodes.summary: Imports externally authored node YAML into a project graph after schema, conflict, and dependency checks, then updates the project index with optional review and dry-run controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Python command-line code organized as explicit, testable functions with pathlib-based filesystem boundaries.

nodes.name: validate_node_yaml
nodes.summary: Validates an imported node document for required fields, accepted values, bounded acceptance criteria, and schema invariants.

nodes.name: check_conflicts
nodes.summary: Checks whether an imported node ID or destination path already exists in the selected project.

nodes.name: check_deps_exist
nodes.summary: Verifies that every declared dependency resolves to an existing node in the target project.

nodes.name: write_node_file
nodes.summary: Persists node file while preserving the canonical import node structure.

nodes.name: patch_project_index
nodes.summary: Adds an imported node to the canonical project index while preserving the project YAML structure.

nodes.name: import_node
nodes.summary: Coordinates validation, conflict checks, dependency checks, human review, file writing, and project-index updates for one imported node.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses command-line input and coordinates the complete import node workflow, including exit status and operator output.

nodes.name: new_node.py
nodes.summary: Provides the interactive node-authoring workflow, from project and dependency selection through YAML rendering, index updates, and post-write validation.
nodes.languageNotes: Python command-line code organized as explicit, testable functions with pathlib-based filesystem boundaries.

nodes.name: list_projects
nodes.summary: Discovers projects used by the new node workflow.

nodes.name: list_node_ids
nodes.summary: Discovers node ids used by the new node workflow.

nodes.name: gather_inspiration_bullets
nodes.summary: Discovers inspiration bullets used by the new node workflow.

nodes.name: manual_text
nodes.summary: Implements the manual text operation within the new node workflow.

nodes.name: pick_from_list
nodes.summary: Implements the pick from list operation within the new node workflow.

nodes.name: pick_many
nodes.summary: Provides a multi-select terminal picker with defaults and optional future dependency entries.

nodes.name: edit_list_items
nodes.summary: Lets the operator add, edit, remove, and reorder a list-valued node field in the terminal workflow.

nodes.name: gather_fields
nodes.summary: Runs the interactive prompts that assemble a complete draft node from project intent, dependencies, criteria, constraints, and execution policy.

nodes.name: render_node_yaml
nodes.summary: Renders node yaml into the stable output form used by new node.

nodes.name: review_screen
nodes.summary: Implements the review screen operation within the new node workflow.

nodes.name: edit_one_field
nodes.summary: Routes a requested node-field revision to the correct bounded editor while preserving the rest of the draft.

nodes.name: write_node_file
nodes.summary: Persists node file while preserving the canonical new node structure.

nodes.name: patch_project_yaml
nodes.summary: Persists project yaml while preserving the canonical new node structure.

nodes.name: run_post_write_validator
nodes.summary: Runs validation after a node and project index are written and restores the prior files when the new state fails validation.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses command-line input and coordinates the complete new node workflow, including exit status and operator output.

nodes.name: test_compliance.py
nodes.summary: Exercises node-authoring and validation compliance, including acceptance-item requirements, scoped editing, and executor-neutral execution modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Python command-line code organized as explicit, testable functions with pathlib-based filesystem boundaries.

nodes.name: tmp_root
nodes.summary: Implements the tmp root operation within the test compliance workflow.

nodes.name: make_placeholder_node
nodes.summary: Builds placeholder node for the test compliance workflow.

nodes.name: make_valid_node
nodes.summary: Builds valid node for the test compliance workflow.

nodes.name: node_errors
nodes.summary: Implements the node errors operation within the test compliance workflow.

nodes.name: test_refuses_empty_acceptance
nodes.summary: Verifies that refuses empty acceptance behaves according to the test compliance contract.

nodes.name: test_prints_validation_findings
nodes.summary: Verifies that prints validation findings behaves according to the test compliance contract.

nodes.name: test_edit_handler_scopes_single_field
nodes.summary: Verifies that edit handler scopes single field behaves according to the test compliance contract.

nodes.name: test_filled_node_passes_validation
nodes.summary: Verifies that filled node passes validation behaves according to the test compliance contract.

nodes.name: test_agent_is_canonical_executor_neutral_mode
nodes.summary: Verifies that agent is canonical executor neutral mode behaves according to the test compliance contract.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses command-line input and coordinates the complete test compliance workflow, including exit status and operator output.

nodes.name: FakeInput
nodes.summary: Deterministic terminal-input double used to drive interactive node-authoring tests without a real TTY.

nodes.name: validate.py
nodes.summary: Validates every graph node against schema and cross-node integrity rules, then renders machine-readable or human-readable findings for CI and operators.
nodes.languageNotes: Python command-line code organized as explicit, testable functions with pathlib-based filesystem boundaries.

nodes.name: validate_node
nodes.summary: Applies field-level schema, status, dependency, acceptance, constraint, and artifact checks to one parsed node document.

nodes.name: cross_node_findings
nodes.summary: Finds missing dependencies, invalid unlock references, and reciprocal graph inconsistencies across a project node set.

nodes.name: iter_node_files
nodes.summary: Discovers node files used by the validate workflow.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Loads graph YAML files, aggregates parse and validation findings, and performs project-wide cross-node checks.

nodes.name: render_human
nodes.summary: Formats validation findings and severity totals as a concise operator-readable report.

nodes.name: render_json
nodes.summary: Serializes validation findings and summary counts into stable machine-readable JSON.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses command-line input and coordinates the complete validate workflow, including exit status and operator output.

nodes.name: Finding
nodes.summary: Immutable validation finding carrying source location, severity, rule identifier, and operator-facing message.

layers.name: Graph Definitions
layers.description: Human-owned project and node YAML that records GDDP intent, dependencies, acceptance criteria, execution constraints, and human-controlled status.

layers.name: Schemas
layers.description: Versioned YAML contracts for GDDP nodes, jobs, events, queue records, results, shape profiles, task packets, and artifact verification.

layers.name: Python Tooling
layers.description: Operator-facing authoring, validation, export, verification, and unified CLI tooling, including its tests and the bin/gddp launcher.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project guidance, architecture and primitive references, operational handoffs, reviewer evidence, and generated Droid wiki material.

layers.name: Shareable Exports
layers.description: Portable project graph bundles that preserve canonical project metadata and ordered node truth for consumers outside this repository.

layers.name: Project Config
layers.description: Repository setup, agent guardrails and hooks, and reusable job, node, and drafting templates that govern work around the graph truth.

tour.title: Graph Truth Doctrine
tour.description: Start with the repository overview and architecture guide to understand why gddp-config owns project intent, schemas, and graph status. Together they establish the filesystem boundaries and data flow that let runtime and executors consume graph truth without owning it.

tour.title: Unified CLI Entry
tour.description: With the ownership model established, follow the operator-facing path from the shell launcher into the unified Python CLI. This surface gathers node authoring, validation, project operations, verification, and read-only runtime job inspection under the config-owned gddp command.
tour.languageLesson: A thin shell launcher can resolve repository-relative paths before transferring control to Python, keeping command discovery stable while the richer CLI remains testable as a Python module.

tour.title: Node Contract
tour.description: The canonical node schema turns the doctrine from Step 1 into a versioned contract for dependencies, acceptance criteria, constraints, evidence, and human-owned status. The template and primitive guide show how authors use that contract consistently when creating graph truth.
tour.languageLesson: YAML schemas separate a readable authoring format from structural validation, allowing human-edited graph records to retain stable field contracts.

tour.title: Project DAGs
tour.description: Building on the node contract, project files index ordered capability graphs while individual node files carry the bounded intent and dependencies. The AA CLI graph illustrates how a foundational common core leads into a widely depended-on dispatch router and executor-specific capabilities.

tour.title: Human Authoring Flow
tour.description: The authoring tools turn graph design into controlled YAML changes: new_node guides creation, batch_fill completes placeholders, and acceptance_items normalizes criteria into stable keyed objects. These tools matter because they help humans revise the source of truth without weakening its structure or reviewability.
tour.languageLesson: Python keeps the interactive authoring flow and normalization rules in reusable modules, so command-line workflows and tests can exercise the same core behavior.

tour.title: Integrity Validation
tour.description: After authoring, validation checks both each YAML record and the relationships across the graph. validate.py enforces schema and cross-node rules, while graph_compare exposes roots, leaves, missing references, and cycles for an operator-readable structural check.

tour.title: Human Status Control
tour.description: Once a graph is structurally sound, node_cli provides the focused list, show, and human-authorized status workflow. Its boundary keeps canonical YAML status distinct from runtime and evaluator evidence while still presenting that evidence to the person making the graph-truth decision.

tour.title: Verification Evidence
tour.description: The verifier evaluates criteria and constraints against repository evidence and produces auditable verdict receipts for human review. The artifact-verification contract formalizes how required outputs are checked, preserving the Step 7 rule that evidence informs status without advancing it automatically.

tour.title: Execution Contracts
tour.description: Config also defines the neutral records consumed across the execution lifecycle: task packets carry bounded intent, events supply triggers, jobs and queue records track attempts, and results return executor evidence. Reading these together shows how the graph remains authoritative while replaceable runtime machinery moves work around it.

tour.title: Shareable Graph Bundles
tour.description: After the canonical graph and contracts are understood, export_graph_bundles packages project metadata with ordered node YAML for external consumers. This is the portability boundary: other tools receive a complete graph snapshot while the editable truth remains in the config repository.

tour.title: Semantic Graph Reading
tour.description: The semantic walkthrough connects the individual surfaces into whole-graph reasoning about dependencies, convergence, validation evidence, and risk. Use it after the concrete tooling and contracts so its project-level analysis can be read in terms of the boundaries established throughout the tour.

tour.title: Boundary Test Suite
tour.description: Finish with the tests that protect the operator and architectural boundaries introduced earlier. They cover unified CLI forwarding, guarded status mutation, authoring compliance, validation behavior, and deterministic verification so future changes can be checked against the intended system shape.
